Fix flaky child_process test: increase timeout 120s → 300s

takes 60s+ per test on constrained runners, and the timeout covers
the full lifecycle including spawn. 300s gives ample headroom.

fix(chatbot): bypass SW cache by fetching listings.json via fetch()

Replace <script src="data/listings.js"> with async fetch('data/listings.json',
{cache:'no-store'}) to fix persistent "0 annonces" issue. Root cause: Service

top-level declarations are not accessible as window.X in all environments.

Data is now stored in chatData object and passed explicitly to immoBot.query().

commit.message:
Fix CrisisWatch ingest: detect broken feed URL, fall back to global RSS

The CrisisWatch-specific RSS (/crisiswatch/rss.xml, /rss/crisiswatch)
now redirects to an HTML page on crisisgroup.org, causing the ingest to
silently return {fetched: 0} because parsers find no <item> blocks.

- Add Content-Type check in fetchAndParseRss(): throw a clear error if
  the server returns text/html instead of RSS/XML so the admin UI shows
  an error rather than silent zeros
- Change default URL from the broken /crisiswatch/rss.xml to the global
  /rss feed which is confirmed working (returns application/rss+xml)
- Add comment noting the breakage date and CRISISWATCH_RSS_URL override

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full edge case handling for all arithmetic modules, bench + production cores

All Spirix FPGA modules now handle the complete edge case chain matching
the Rust reference spec: undefined passthrough, zero/infinity/exploded/
vanished detection, and spec-defined undefined prefix results.

Bench modules (FRAC=25, EXP=8): addsub, addsub_pipe2, multiply,
multiply_pipe2, fma, divide, divide_iter, divmod_nr, sqrt_nr, sqrt_iter.
Production cores (parameterized): addsub, multiply, divide, sqrt, nr_divsqrt.

Edge case test vector generators added for all ops. Paper updated with

commit.message:
Fix SGLang multi-turn failure: canonicalize message ordering

SGLang enforces strict OpenAI-style ordering where all system messages
must precede non-system messages. Two bugs caused system messages to
appear after user/assistant/tool messages:

1. execution.rs: learning hint was .push()ed to end of message vec
2. api/mod.rs: no safety net for misplaced system messages

Fixes:
- Merge learning hints into existing system message (execution.rs)
- Add canonicalize_message_order() in API layer as defense-in-depth
- 10 unit tests covering all edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial release of adaptive-classifier v0.1.0

Self-building hybrid text classifier combining FAISS embedding search
with LLM fallback and automatic feedback loop. LLM results feed back
into the index so accuracy improves and LLM costs decrease over time.

Core library:
- AdaptiveClassifier orchestrator with hybrid classify pipeline
- Pluggable LLM providers: Anthropic, Vertex AI, Bedrock, callable
- Pluggable vector store protocol with FAISS default
- SentenceTransformer embedding provider (all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
- Taxonomy support: nested dict, flat paths, JSON, YAML files
- Text normalizer with abbreviation expansion, code/amount stripping
- Index persistence (.faiss + .meta.json) with auto-save/feedback

Benchmark (example/):
- Financial transaction benchmark against 4.5M record HuggingFace dataset
- Three-pass evaluation: embedding-only, hybrid+LLM, post-feedback
- Multi-run learning curve showing LLM usage decreasing over time
- 42% embedding-only -> 90% hybrid -> 84% post-feedback accuracy

Project infrastructure:
- CI workflow: pytest across Python 3.10-3.13 with ruff linting
- Publish workflow: PyPI trusted publishing on GitHub release
- uv support with dependency-groups for dev tooling
- README with badges, usage examples, benchmark results, release docs
- CONTRIBUTING.md and CHANGELOG.md for open-source readiness
- Devcontainer configuration for codespaces

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Kenya Law scraper — full PDF download pipeline

- Downloads actual PDFs (not just metadata) from new.kenyalaw.org
- 314K+ judgments, 490+ legislation, 8K+ gazettes
- Hierarchical traversal: court → station → year → month (bypasses 10-page limit)
- 20 workers, 0.1s delay — optimised for VPS throughput
- SQLite WAL checkpoint for resume after any disruption
- Organized storage: case_law/{COURT}/{station}/{year}/{month}/{citation}.pdf
- Dockerfile + Railway-ready deployment

commit.message:
Major assessment UX + email wiring overhaul

Auto-advance behavior:
- Steps 2, 7, 8 (single-select choices): click auto-advances after 300ms with green pulse animation
- Step 5 (yes/no email): both answers auto-advance
- Step 9 (timeline): click calls submitAssessment() directly
- Steps 3 & 4 (yes/no with conditional field): No auto-advances; Yes reveals URL/CRM field + Continue button
- Step 6 (multi-select social) + Step 1 (text): keep Continue button

Back navigation:
- All steps 2-9 have subtle "← Back" text link (not a chunky button)
- Backspace / ArrowLeft keys also go back unless focus is in an input

Keyboard:
- Enter advances only on text/multi-select steps (1, 3, 4, 6)
- Auto-advance steps excluded from Enter handler to prevent double-fire

Email on submit (api/assessment-submit.js):
- Sends branded HTML email to prospect (tier, features, cost comparison, Calendly CTA)
- Sends plain-text lead summary to andrew@peakagentai.com (all answers, urgency flag)
- Uses Resend with hello@bizstack.vip sender; gracefully skips if RESEND_API_KEY not set
- Frontend fires API in background — never blocks results page render
